---
title: 'VAE: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes'
description: Encode data into a Gaussian latent and decode it back; maximise the ELBO
  (reconstruction − KL) and backprop through sampling via the reparameterisation trick.
categories:
- Generative Models
---

## Objective

| Term | Description |
|---|---|
| Data point $x$ | Observed data from the unknown distribution $p(x)$ |
| Latent code $z$ | Lower-dimensional vector capturing factors of variation |
| Marginal likelihood $p_\theta(x)=\int p_\theta(x\mid z)p(z)\,dz$ | Intractable — maximise its ELBO instead |
| Prior $p(z)=\mathcal{N}(0,I)$ | Fixed design choice |
| True posterior $p_\theta(z\mid x)$ | Intractable |
| Encoder (approx. posterior) $q_\phi(z\mid x)=\mathcal{N}(\mu_\phi(x),\sigma_\phi^2(x)I)$ | Inference model, weights $\phi$ |
| Decoder (likelihood) $p_\theta(x\mid z)=\mathcal{N}(\mu_\theta(z),\sigma_\theta^2(z)I)$ | Generative model, weights $\theta$ |

Unlike a plain autoencoder, which maps each input to a single latent point, the VAE represents data as a **Gaussian** in latent space. The prior forces the encoder to arrange codes so the decoder sees the whole region — without it there are empty "holes" the decoder never learned to decode.

## ELBO

$$
\mathbb{E}_{q_\phi(z\mid x)}\Big[\log\frac{p(x,z)}{q_\phi(z\mid x)}\Big] = \underbrace{\mathbb{E}_{q_\phi(z\mid x)}[\log p_\theta(x\mid z)]}_{\text{reconstruction}} - \underbrace{D_{KL}\big(q_\phi(z\mid x)\,\|\,p(z)\big)}_{\text{prior matching}}.
$$

With only the reconstruction term the encoder collapses to a deterministic point ($q_\phi\to\delta(z-\mu_\phi(x))$, variance $\to 0$). The KL term keeps it stochastic and close to the prior.

## Reparameterisation trick

Sampling $z\sim q_\phi(z\mid x)$ blocks gradients. Rewrite it as a deterministic function of independent noise:

$$
z = \mu_\phi(x) + \sigma_\phi(x)\odot\epsilon, \qquad \epsilon\sim\mathcal{N}(0,I),
$$

so $\nabla_\phi \mathbb{E}_{\epsilon}[f(z)] = \mathbb{E}_{\epsilon}[\nabla_\phi f(z)]$ and backprop flows through $\mu_\phi,\sigma_\phi$.

## Architecture

![VAE encoder–decoder](/images/notes/vae-1.png)

## Code

```python
class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, Encoder, Decoder):
        super().__init__()
        self.Encoder, self.Decoder = Encoder, Decoder

    def reparameterization(self, mean, var):
        epsilon = torch.randn_like(var).to(DEVICE)
        return mean + var * epsilon                 # reparameterisation trick

    def forward(self, x):
        mean, log_var = self.Encoder(x)
        z = self.reparameterization(mean, torch.exp(0.5 * log_var))
        return self.Decoder(z), mean, log_var

def loss_function(x, x_hat, mean, log_var):
    recon = F.binary_cross_entropy(x_hat, x, reduction='sum')
    kld   = -0.5 * torch.sum(1 + log_var - mean.pow(2) - log_var.exp())
    return recon + kld
```

## References

- [arXiv:1312.6114 — Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes](https://arxiv.org/abs/1312.6114)
- [arXiv:1906.02691 — An Introduction to Variational Autoencoders](https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.02691)
- [arXiv:1606.05908 — Tutorial on Variational Autoencoders](https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.05908)
- [Lilian Weng — From Autoencoder to Beta-VAE](https://lilianweng.github.io/posts/2018-08-12-vae/)
- [Video — Deepia](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qJeaCHQ1k2w)
